ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Bánh Xèo. 'Pancake food product' (FOODON_03302051) is the closest defensible parent class covering savoury rice-flour pancakes.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Pancakes) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for Vietnamese rice-flour sizzling pancake with prawn filling.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept found for Bánh Xèo or Vietnamese sizzling pancake. SNOMED CT does not cover this dish at the required specificity.

serving.description: 1 pancake (approximately half a large pancake as served)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Bánh Xèo is approximately 200 g, representing one whole medium-sized sizzling pancake with prawn, bean sprout, and pork filling as typically served at a Vietnamese restaurant.

nutrient_source.title: Banh Xeo (Vietnamese Sizzling Pancake) — nutrient profile estimated from FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table and USDA FoodData Central component data for rice flour pancake with prawn and pork filling
nutrient_source.organization: FAO/INFOODS Southeast Asian Food Composition Table (2020); USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for Bánh Xèo with prawn filling as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated by combining FAO/INFOODS SEA data for rice flour, coconut milk, and bean sprouts with USDA SR Legacy data for prawns and pork belly, weighted by typical recipe proportions (rice flour batter ~45%, prawn ~20%, pork belly ~15%, bean sprouts ~15%, oil ~5%). Values represent a reasonable mid-range estimate for a restaurant-prepared version. Confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Rice flour-based pancake / crepe GI analogous estimate; reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Bánh Xèo with prawn filling. GI estimated at 52 based on the closest analogous food: rice flour-based pancake/crepe (GI ~52, low category) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. The rice flour batter dominates the glycemic response; prawn and pork filling contribute negligible glycemic load. Glycemic load per 200 g serving = (52 × 18.5 g carbohydrate per 100g × 2) / 100 = 19.2 g GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~320 mg per 100 g; ~640 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Bánh Xèo to occasional consumption and request reduced fish sauce in the dipping sauce (nước chấm), as a single 200 g serving contributes approximately 640 mg sodium — about 28% of the 2,300 mg daily limit recommended by clinical guidelines.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~19 GL per serving) from rice flour batter
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Bánh Xèo in controlled portions, as the rice flour batter produces a moderate glycemic load of approximately 19 per standard serving; pairing with non-starchy vegetables and limiting the sweet dipping sauce can help attenuate postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from pork belly and coconut milk (~3.8 g per 100 g) and moderate sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should be mindful that Bánh Xèo contains approximately 3.8 g saturated fat per 100 g from coconut milk and pork belly, contributing to LDL-raising saturated fat intake; choosing a prawn-only filling and reducing oil used in frying can lower the saturated fat and overall cardiovascular risk profile.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bánh Xèo (Vietnamese Prawn Sizzling Pancake) is a crispy, golden Vietnamese savoury pancake made from a batter of rice flour, coconut milk, water, and turmeric powder — the turmeric giving the pancake its characteristic bright yellow colour. The name 'Bánh Xèo' literally means 'sizzling cake', referring to the loud sizzling sound produced when the batter is poured into a very hot oiled skillet. The pancake is folded in half over a filling of prawns, pork belly slices, and bean sprouts, then pan-fried until the edges are lacy and crisp while the interior remains slightly chewy. It is traditionally served with fresh lettuce leaves, Vietnamese herbs (mint, perilla, fish mint), and a sweet-sour fish sauce dipping sauce (nước chấm). In Singapore, Bánh Xèo is served at Vietnamese restaurants and is popular as a sharing starter or light main course. Nutritionally, a 200 g serving provides approximately 370 kcal, 17 g protein, 18 g fat, and 37 g carbohydrate. The dish has a moderate glycemic index (~52) and a moderate glycemic load (~19 per serving) due to the rice flour base. Sodium content is moderate to high (~640 mg per serving) largely from fish sauce in the dipping condiment. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans due to prawn and pork content, and is not certified halal. Clinically, portion control and dipping sauce moderation are the primary dietary considerations for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a Vietnamese sizzling pancake?
What is the GI of Vietnamese rice pancake?
What is in a Vietnamese prawn pancake?
What does Bánh Xèo taste like?

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Bánh Xèo with prawn filling as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from FAO/INFOODS SEA and USDA SR Legacy component data weighted by typical recipe proportions. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for rice flour pancake. Confidence is medium rather than high because no exact match was found in any primary approved source.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the FAO/INFOODS SEA or USDA SR Legacy component sources used for this estimation. All other -1 fields have been replaced with estimated values where component data permitted. Nutrient values represent a mid-range estimate for a restaurant-prepared Bánh Xèo with prawn, pork belly, and bean sprout filling; actual values will vary by restaurant recipe, oil quantity, and dipping sauce inclusion. The dish is classified under 'Fish and seafood' food group consistent with the input record, as prawns are the primary protein and the dish is marketed as a prawn pancake. The cuisine is Vietnamese (served in Singapore at a Vietnamese restaurant); multilingual names follow the Singaporean dish rules (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) plus Vietnamese as the dish's origin language. GI category 'low' is confirmed: glycemic_index = 52, which is ≤ 55 per the Veritas threshold rule.
